title: Non-convex Hypersurfaces And Robust Heterodimensional Cycles
speaker: Julian Chaidez
abstract:
A closed hypersurface in a contact manifold is convex if it admits a transverse contact vector field. A closed hypersurface is robustly non-convex if it cannot be smoothly approximated by a convex hypersurface. The existence of such hypersurfaces was a longstanding open problem, resolved recently by myself using tools from partially hyperbolic dynamics.
In this talk, I will explain the relationship between robust non-convexity and the presence of a structure called a  heterodimensional cycle in the characteristic foliation of the hypersurface. As an application, one can prove that any closed hypersurface in any contact manifold can be perturbed in the C0
-topology to a robustly non-convex hypersurface. This substantially generalizes and simplifies my previous work. This talk covers joint work with my PhD student Michael Huang (USC).

proof_stages.label: Convexity problem and main density theorem
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines convex hypersurfaces and CK-robust non-convexity, recalls known density and non-density results, and states the new theorem that C2-robustly non-convex hypersurfaces are C0-dense for arbitrary hypersurface topology.

proof_stages.label: Characteristic foliation of a contact hypersurface
proof_stages.summary: The speaker introduces the characteristic foliation as the singular line field defined from the kernel of dα on ξ ∩ TΣ, and fixes terminology for characteristic vector fields and flows.

proof_stages.label: Positive and negative Liouville subsets
proof_stages.summary: A subset of the hypersurface is called Liouville when some contact form restricts to a Liouville form near it, and an orientation distinguishes positive from negative Liouville behavior.

proof_stages.label: Invariant-set sign rigidity and hyperbolic criteria
proof_stages.summary: Compact invariant Liouville sets cannot be both positive and negative, and transitive hyperbolic invariant sets are Liouville with sign controlled by their stable index, using an ergodic/closing-lemma criterion.

proof_stages.label: Convexity obstruction from a forbidden heteroclinic
proof_stages.summary: For a convex hypersurface, the dividing set separates positive and negative Liouville domains, so a heteroclinic trajectory from a negative Liouville invariant set to a positive one cannot exist.

proof_stages.label: Robust plus-minus heterodimensional cycles
proof_stages.summary: Because ordinary closed-orbit heteroclinics are fragile, the obstruction is upgraded to robust heterodimensional cycles between larger hyperbolic invariant sets, a mechanism known in smooth dynamics and closely related to blenders.

proof_stages.label: Construction of dense robust non-convexity
proof_stages.summary: Every hypersurface is C0-perturbed to acquire a possibly fragile plus-minus heterodimensional cycle, then C∞-perturbed via a contact version of unfolding results from dynamics to make the cycle robust, yielding C2-robust non-convexity.

proof_stages.label: Generic convex-or-cycle conjecture
proof_stages.summary: The speaker conjectures that on a C2-dense open set every hypersurface is either convex or has a plus-minus heterodimensional cycle, motivated by the smooth-dynamics dichotomy between Axiom A behavior and heterodimensional cycles.
